pattern: The h-index is the largest h such that at least h papers each have h or more citations, found by counting how many papers clear each threshold using a bucketed tally capped at the paper count.
hints: Sorting the citations in descending order lets you scan for the point where the citation value can no longer keep pace with the paper rank.
Citations above the number of papers cannot raise the h-index further, so a count bucketed by citation value and capped at n suffices.

Bucket papers by citation count (anything at least n falls in the top bucket), then accumulate counts from the highest bucket downward and return the first h where the running total of papers reaches h.

edge_inputs.description: mixed citations giving an h-index of three
small set with an h-index of one
all zeros, no paper is cited so h-index is zero
single highly cited paper still gives h-index one
all papers cited far above n, h-index capped at n
uniform citations equal to the count
one big and many zeros, h-index is one

stress.description: random citations spanning the full value range to stress the bucket cap at n
